old_claim.note: Scientific Societies provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No scientific-society dependency remains because the experimental-controls node is dated 1553, while the evidence-locked scientific-society category begins in 1560.

source_shape.source_locator: CORDIS explicitly dates the Academia Secretorum Naturae foundation to Naples in 1560.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The earliest directly supported society in the category appears after the current 1553 controls anchor.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later institution cannot enable an earlier documented practice, and the existing controls source supplies no alternative society connection.

ontology_before: A placeholder 1500 society date masked a seven-year chronology inversion into experimental controls.
ontology_after: The earlier controls claim no longer depends on a later-founded institutional category.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: experimental_controls no longer directly depends on scientific_societies.
would_reject_if: Strong evidence moved the scientific-society category before 1553 and directly tied that institution to the scoped experimental controls.
short_reason: The controls date precedes the first directly supported scientific society.
